description_long: This is a tall, cylindrical bottle with a slightly flared base and a narrow neck that features a screw thread for a cap. The bottle is predominantly a pale, almost white color with visible blue stains and markings, suggesting it has been used or exposed to the elements. There are two sets of horizontal ridges encircling the bottle, one near the base and one near the top, providing both structural reinforcement and grip. Faint, partially worn text and graphics are visible on the body, indicating it may have once contained a branded product. The surface appears matte and somewhat rough, with evidence of dirt or residue in some areas. The bottle is made of a hard material, likely plastic, and is hollow inside.
description: A tall, pale bottle with blue stains, ridged bands, and faint worn text, made of hard plastic with a screw cap neck.
description_view_0: Front view shows the bottle's screw neck, blue stains, and most visible text and graphics on the body.
description_view_1: This view highlights more blue staining near the top and additional faded markings, with less visible text.
description_view_2: The back view reveals minimal text, mostly plain surface, and some blue discoloration near the neck.
description_view_3: Side view shows the bottle's cylindrical shape, ridges, and a large blue stain near the top, with little visible text.
attribute_label_or_branding: Faint, partially worn blue text and graphics are visible on the body, but specific branding is not clearly legible.
attribute_damage_or_deformation: Visible blue stains, faded text, and some surface discoloration; no major dents or cracks.

license_info.creator_display_name: Archeoplastica: il museo dei rifiuti spiaggiati
